commit.message:
fix: harden entity extraction flow — remove eager summary generation, deduplicate logic

- Remove generateAndSaveSummary() call from Telegram /save entity loop (was firing
  1 OpenAI call per entity, turning 3s saves into 30s). Summaries now generated
  on-demand via /entity command only.
- Extract upsertEntitiesAndLink to lib/entities.ts (was duplicated in 3 places)
- Merge entity structured data instead of overwriting (prevents data loss when a
  later note mentions an entity without role/org info)
- Only invalidate summary_updated_at when structured data actually changed
- Add ignoreDuplicates guard on knowledge_links insert
- Normalize entity names to lowercase before storage (fixes Alice vs alice dedup)
- Fix PUT ordering: run AI extraction before deleting old links (reduces consistency
  gap from ~5s to ~50ms)
- Add migration: unique index on entities(user_id,lower(name)) and
  knowledge_links(knowledge_id,entity_id)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Child theme: littlenamebox-theme with Google Fonts + GeneratePress parent enqueue

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

signet: replace hand-rolled crypto in nip46_server.c with library calls

Remove ~1700 lines of reimplemented BIP340 Schnorr signing, NIP-04
encrypt/decrypt, SHA-256 tagged hashing, secp256k1 EC operations, event
serialization, and hex encoding. Replace with calls to libnostr
(nostr_event_sign, nostr_event_serialize_compact), nips/nip04
(nostr_nip04_encrypt/decrypt), and nips/nip46 (request_parse,
response_build_ok/err).

The module is now purely an orchestration layer: replay check → decrypt
→ parse → policy → execute → audit → encrypt → publish.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Remove standard environments - use only custom environment uploads

Fixes initialization error: "Cannot access 'standardEnvironments' before initialization"

Changes:
- Removed API calls that fetched standard environments from /api/config and /api/environments
- Disabled setupEnvironments() function (now commented out)
- Removed standardEnvironments variable declaration
- Updated renderCustomEnvironmentsList() to only show custom environments
- Updated exportEnvironmentsAsCSV() to only export custom environments
- Added helpful message when no environments are configured
- Users now must add environments manually (➕) or upload CSV (📤)

This simplifies the application to only use localStorage-based custom environments.

* feat: rework gallery to card stack with slanted side cards

Center image sits on top (z-10), prev/next cards peek from behind

card or using arrows/dots animates the transition (500ms ease-out).
No scroll-based logic — pure state-driven with CSS transitions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add touch swipe and fix card stack overflow

Add touch swipe support (50px threshold) on the center card for
mobile navigation. Reduce side card size (80% width, 3deg rotation,
0.9 scale) and add px-4 padding to prevent cards from protruding
outside the container.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: fluid drag-to-swipe for card stack gallery

Center card follows finger in real-time during drag. Side cards
interpolate rotation/scale/overlay toward center as you drag toward
them. Releases use velocity + distance threshold to decide snap vs
bounce-back. Easing uses cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) for a
natural spring-like settle. Rubber-band effect at edges.

commit.message:
Remove empty placeholder elements (pipeline demo, comparison slider, problem detector)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add 18 new rules for MCP, agentic execution, config files, and hooks

New rule categories covering emerging AI security attack surfaces:

- MCP Server Security (6 rules): command injection, SSRF, tool poisoning,
  unsanitized returns, credential leaks, hardcoded config secrets
- Agent Config File Security (5 rules): hidden Unicode in AI config files,
  IDE executable path hijacking, bypass permissions, API URL overrides,
  auto-enable MCP servers
- Agentic Code Execution (3 rules): LLM output to eval/exec/subprocess,
  dangerous LangChain utilities, unbounded agent loops
- Extended Hooks (4 rules): unconditional permission allow, stop hook
  infinite loops, DNS exfiltration, wget-pipe-bash RCE

Informed by OWASP Agentic Top 10, MCP security research, Pillar Security
Rules File Backdoor, CVE-2025-55284, IDEsaster, and Trail of Bits guidance.

58 rules, 102 sub-rules. All validated, 102/102 tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
signet: remove Vault client, migrate to SQLCipher architecture

Delete vault_client.c/h and remove all HashiCorp Vault dependencies from
Signet. This is the first step in migrating from Vault-backed key custody
to SQLCipher + mlock'd hot key cache per the new design spec.

Changes:
- Delete vault_client.c (575 lines) and vault_client.h
- Rewrite key_store.h/c as stub interface (no Vault dependency)
- Remove all Vault config fields from signet_config.h/c
- Replace health_server vault_reachable with db_open, agents_active,
  cache_entries, relay_count fields matching design spec
- Remove Vault policy store backend from policy_store.h/c
- Remove Vault init/teardown from signetd_main.c
- Remove libcurl dependency from meson.build, CMakeLists.txt, meson.options
- Update test_signet_smoke.c to use new interfaces
- Rewrite README.md for SQLCipher + hot cache architecture
- Rewrite INTEGRATION.md for Nostr-native management protocol
- Update signet.toml.example to match design spec format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve all critical bugs found in code review and integration testing

Extractor fixes:
- Jira: migrate to /search/jql endpoint (old /search removed by Atlassian),
  add pagination to ListProjects, fix combined CreatedAfter+UpdatedAfter filter
- Asana: add next_page cursor pagination for ListProjects and ExtractProject tasks
- Trello: add before-cursor pagination for boards with >1000 cards
- Monday: add explicit Name column to schema (implicit field not in board.Columns),
  skip name-type columns to avoid duplicates
- Notion: fetch DB title via /databases/{id} instead of using raw UUID as sheet name,
  add typed column schema (date/checkbox/select/people), ensure title prop is first
- Airtable: extract attachment URL+filename+size into Row.Attachments instead of
  silently dropping all attachments; skip attachment fields as cell values
- Wrike: fix task fields URL to only request description (responsibles/dates not
  available via folder tasks endpoint); remove Assignees column until endpoint
  supports it; fix resolveContactEmails for future use

Loader fixes:
- Append ' (text)' suffix to contact-type column names to prevent Smartsheet
  auto-classifying them as CONTACT_LIST (which rejects plain string values with 1235)
- Map both suffixed and original column names in colMap so extractor cells resolve
- normalizeCellValue: convert []string and []interface{} slices to comma-joined
  strings before sending to Smartsheet (fixes errorCode 1008 on multi-value cells)
- readAPIError: include response body in all error messages for debuggability
- Fetch actual column types in createSheetResponse for future use

CLI fixes:
- Skip interactive token/source prompts when --yes flag is set (was overwriting
  flags with empty string in non-TTY background processes)
- Add guard errors for missing --source-token and --smartsheet-token
- deduplicateProjectColumns: add before running migration
- attachmentHTTPClient: use 60s timeout instead of http.DefaultClient (no timeout)

Rate limiter:
- ratelimit.Wait() now accepts optional context.Context for cancellation propagation

Other:
- Fix /migrate binary gitignore pattern to /migrate (root-only) so cmd/migrate/
  is no longer excluded
- Fix TestJiraListProjects mock to return isLast:true preventing infinite loop

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update README + switch to Apache 2.0 license

Major README rewrite with push headers, WS format, use cases.

Fix road overlap at shared vertices: shorten endpoints and layer by building owner

messy overlap where different players' roads meet. Roads under a player's

so their roads visually dominate at contested junctions.

commit.message:
feat: context-aware recall — auto-infer context from query and boost matching traits

Implement Tulving's Encoding Specificity Principle: recall automatically infers
user context (work/personal/social/learning) from query embedding via prototype
matching, then applies soft score boost (0~0.10×confidence) to matching traits.

- Add ContextService with bilingual prototype vectors + keyword fallback
- Extend scored_search with context_match_bonus SQL (whitelist-validated)
- Extend recall() return with inferred_context + context_confidence
- 52 test cases (unit/integration/e2e)
- Zero new dependencies, <1ms overhead, non-breaking API change
- Bump version to 0.9.12

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

exist. The web UI now reports syntax errors alongside lint errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(restore): fix /restore silent crash + extract run_restore() shared

- /restore crashed silently because `os` was not imported — fixed
- Extracted `run_restore()` in cmd_backup.py (same pattern as run_backup)
- Both CLI `syne restore` and Telegram `/restore` now use run_restore()
- No more duplicated restore logic

v0.27.8

messages, and README to explicitly cover both tools.
